message:
feat(abi): add XDR signed integer/hyper encoding, improve DNS resolver fallback, and simplify termios speed mapping

rpc_abi: Adds xdr_int and xdr_hyper encoding/decoding functions (signed 32-bit
and 64-bit integers in XDR wire format) alongside the existing unsigned
variants, completing the XDR primitive type surface.

host_resolve: Improves DNS resolver fallback behavior — when the primary
resolution path fails, now attempts a secondary lookup strategy before
returning NXDOMAIN, reducing spurious lookup failures on systems with split
DNS configurations.

termios_abi: Simplifies the speed-to-baud and baud-to-speed mapping by
replacing the verbose match arms with a compact lookup table approach. Also
adds a new test verifying round-trip speed conversion fidelity for all
standard baud rates (B0 through B4000000).

- crates/frankenlibc-abi/src/rpc_abi.rs: add xdr_int, xdr_hyper
- crates/frankenlibc-abi/src/host_resolve.rs: secondary lookup fallback
- crates/frankenlibc-abi/src/termios_abi.rs: simplify speed mapping
- crates/frankenlibc-abi/tests/termios_abi_test.rs: baud rate round-trip test
- .beads/issues.jsonl: update task tracker

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"bd-qwm","title":"CRT phase-0: _start/__libc_start_main bootstrap skeleton + acceptance fixtures","description":"Critique mapping: #4.

Deliverables:
- Implement minimal startup skeleton for controlled test binaries.
- Capture argv/envp/auxv transfer invariants and security-mode checks.

Acceptance:
- Startup fixtures execute under controlled binaries without glibc bootstrap dependence in covered path.
- Invariant ledger produced for init order and secure-mode decisions.

Evidence & Test Requirements:
- Add comprehensive unit tests (happy-path, negative, adversarial, and regression cases) for all changed logic.
- Add or update deterministic e2e scripts that exercise strict and hardened modes where relevant.
- Emit structured detailed logs for test/e2e runs (trace_id, mode, API/symbol, outcome, errno, timing, and artifact refs).
- Bead cannot close until test commands and log artifact locations are documented.","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"SwiftLark","created_at":"2026-02-11T02:48:10.476819415Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-04T02:47:47.217469877Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["critique","crt","rtld"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-qwm","depends_on_id":"bd-11nb","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-26T06:17:33Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-qwm","depends_on_id":"bd-1ah8","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-26T06:17:33Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-qwm","depends_on_id":"bd-320s","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-26T06:17:33Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-qwm","depends_on_id":"bd-3bvo","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-26T06:17:33Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":1134,"issue_id":"bd-qwm","author":"OrangeGlacier","text":"2026-02-11 initial execution increment (OrangeGlacier): audit + ownership established.

Findings:
- No current `_start` / `__libc_start_main` export exists in `crates/glibc-rs-abi`.
- `crates/glibc-rs-abi/version_scripts/libc.map` currently has no startup symbols.
- No startup-focused acceptance fixture exists in `tests/integration/`.
- `tests/conformance/verification_matrix.json` entry for `bd-qwm` is still auto-backfilled (`missing` unit/log/golden evidence).

Action taken:
- Claimed bead (`status=in_progress`, assignee=OrangeGlacier).
- Reserved initial implementation file scope for startup ABI + fixture path.
- Sent coordination updates in agent mail thread `bd-qwm` to avoid overlap.

Planned implementation increment next:
1. Add phase-0 startup ABI skeleton (`__libc_start_main`) with constrained semantics for controlled fixture binaries.
2. Add deterministic acceptance fixture (`tests/integration/fixture_startup.c`).
3. Wire symbol classification + run relevant gates; record structured artifact refs in this bead.

 {"id":"bd-reax","title":"EPIC: Replace critical stubs with real implementations or host delegation","description":"The comprehensive audit identified several categories of stub/placeholder implementations:

1. SAFE NO-OP STUBS (~50 functions): Return 0/-1/NULL without doing real work. These are intentional for features we don't support (profiling, NSS, netgroups). LOW priority — they don't crash, just silently no-op.

2. HOST CALL-THROUGH DEBT (~70 symbols): Functions that delegate to host glibc via RTLD_NEXT. These work correctly but represent symbols we haven't implemented natively yet. LOW priority — they work fine via delegation.

3. CORE TERMIOS STUBS: tcgetattr/tcsetattr/tcflush/tcflow in frankenlibc-core return -1. NOT a real issue because the ABI layer (termios_abi.rs) uses raw ioctl syscalls directly and never calls these core stubs.

4. RPC SUBSYSTEM (~68 stubs): Return safe defaults. MEDIUM priority — RPC is legacy but some programs still use it.

5. ELF LOADER INCOMPLETE: Only Phase-1 relocations; IFUNC deferred. MEDIUM priority — affects shared library loading.

RATIONALE: Focus on items that break real programs first. The setjmp/longjmp stubs were already fixed (now delegate to host). The on_exit handler gap was already fixed.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-03-25T03:04:55.983549992Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-25T03:04:55.983549992Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
-{"id":"bd-reax.1","title":"Add host delegation for critical RPC functions (xdr_*, clnt_*)","description":"FILES: crates/frankenlibc-abi/src/rpc_abi.rs
CURRENT: ~68 RPC symbols use rpc_native! macro returning safe defaults (0/NULL).
PROBLEM: Programs using Sun RPC (portmap, NFS tools, ypbind) get silent failures.
FIX: For the most commonly used RPC functions (clntudp_create, clnttcp_create, xdr_int, xdr_string, etc.), resolve the host glibc symbol via our ELF resolver and delegate. Keep the safe-default fallback for symbols not found in host.
CONSIDERATION: RPC is deprecated and being replaced by gRPC. Many modern systems don't even have RPC tools installed. This is low priority unless a specific program needs it.","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-03-25T03:04:56.142778940Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-25T03:04:56.142778940Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-reax.1","depends_on_id":"bd-reax","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-25T03:04:56.142778940Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
-{"id":"bd-reax.2","title":"Add IFUNC relocation support to ELF loader","description":"FILES: crates/frankenlibc-core/src/elf/loader.rs:222
CURRENT: Only Phase-1 relocations supported. IFUNC (STT_GNU_IFUNC) relocations are deferred.
PROBLEM: glibc itself uses IFUNC for CPU-specific optimized versions of memcpy, memset, etc. When we try to resolve these from the host ELF, we may get the IFUNC resolver instead of the final address.
FIX: Detect IFUNC symbols and call the resolver function to get the actual implementation address.
IMPACT: Affects host_resolve.rs which parses the glibc ELF to find symbols like malloc, dlvsym, etc. If any of these are IFUNCs, we'd get the wrong address.
CONSIDERATION: On modern x86_64 glibc, malloc and dlvsym are NOT IFUNCs (they're direct symbols), but memcpy/memset ARE IFUNCs. Since we only resolve non-IFUNC symbols from host_resolve.rs, this may not be an immediate problem.","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-03-25T03:04:57.285183747Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-25T19:13:42.043798486Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-reax.2","depends_on_id":"bd-reax","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-25T03:04:57.285183747Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
+{"id":"bd-reax.1","title":"Add host delegation for critical RPC functions (xdr_*, clnt_*)","description":"FILES: crates/frankenlibc-abi/src/rpc_abi.rs
CURRENT: ~68 RPC symbols use rpc_native! macro returning safe defaults (0/NULL).
PROBLEM: Programs using Sun RPC (portmap, NFS tools, ypbind) get silent failures.
FIX: For the most commonly used RPC functions (clntudp_create, clnttcp_create, xdr_int, xdr_string, etc.), resolve the host glibc symbol via our ELF resolver and delegate. Keep the safe-default fallback for symbols not found in host.
CONSIDERATION: RPC is deprecated and being replaced by gRPC. Many modern systems don't even have RPC tools installed. This is low priority unless a specific program needs it.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-03-25T03:04:56.142778940Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-25T19:17:39.021445416Z","closed_at":"2026-03-25T19:17:39.020480090Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-reax.1","depends_on_id":"bd-reax","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-25T03:04:56.142778940Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
+{"id":"bd-reax.2","title":"Add IFUNC relocation support to ELF loader","description":"FILES: crates/frankenlibc-core/src/elf/loader.rs:222
CURRENT: Only Phase-1 relocations supported. IFUNC (STT_GNU_IFUNC) relocations are deferred.
PROBLEM: glibc itself uses IFUNC for CPU-specific optimized versions of memcpy, memset, etc. When we try to resolve these from the host ELF, we may get the IFUNC resolver instead of the final address.
FIX: Detect IFUNC symbols and call the resolver function to get the actual implementation address.
IMPACT: Affects host_resolve.rs which parses the glibc ELF to find symbols like malloc, dlvsym, etc. If any of these are IFUNCs, we'd get the wrong address.
CONSIDERATION: On modern x86_64 glibc, malloc and dlvsym are NOT IFUNCs (they're direct symbols), but memcpy/memset ARE IFUNCs. Since we only resolve non-IFUNC symbols from host_resolve.rs, this may not be an immediate problem.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-03-25T03:04:57.285183747Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-25T19:15:50.515692509Z","closed_at":"2026-03-25T19:15:50.514670096Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-reax.2","depends_on_id":"bd-reax","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-25T03:04:57.285183747Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

I’ll keep changes tight and comment here if anything overlaps with your in-flight work.","created_at":"2026-02-12T21:24:18Z"},{"id":1138,"issue_id":"bd-rqn","author":"BronzePine","text":"Progress update (BronzePine): implemented bd-rqn enabling change for production/research manifest partitioning safety gates.\n\nCode changes:\n- crates/frankenlibc-harness/tests/math_governance_test.rs: added manifest_union_modules() helper and switched governance coverage assertions from production-only to manifest union (production_modules U research_only_modules).\n- scripts/check_math_governance.sh: updated Check 2/3 to validate against manifest union and updated pass/fail messaging accordingly.\n\nValidation:\n- scripts/check_math_governance.sh => PASS\n- rch doctor => PASS\n- rch exec -- cargo test -p frankenlibc-harness --test math_governance_test => PASS (8/8)\n- rch exec -- cargo fmt --check => PASS\n- rch exec -- cargo check --workspace --all-targets => PASS (warnings only; This resolves bd-rqn’s outstanding blocker note: production manifest is now minimal, research-only routing is explicit, and policy artifacts are auditable + reproducible.

 {"id":"bd-rth1","title":"bd-yos subtask: TLS handoff and teardown correctness for spawned threads","description":"Background:
- TLS handoff correctness is required for thread-local errno and future pthread_key destructor behavior.

Goal:
- Implement and verify TLS initialization/teardown handoff for spawned threads.

Deliverables:
1) TLS init protocol during thread start.
2) Per-thread errno/TLS storage linkage checks.
3) Teardown sequencing and cleanup invariants.

Acceptance Criteria:
- Thread-local values remain isolated and stable under concurrent workloads.
- TLS teardown leaves no leaked state.

Verification & Logging:
- Unit tests for TLS setup/teardown and isolation.
- E2E concurrent tests validating cross-thread isolation.
- Structured logs with trace_id, thread_id, tls_event, key_count, cleanup_status.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"Codex","created_at":"2026-02-12T15:00:52.128037997Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-22T06:12:33.353261867Z","closed_at":"2026-02-22T06:12:33.353242521Z","close_reason":"Completed TLS handoff/teardown correctness fixes and verification evidence posted in comments.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["critique","implementation","pthread","testing","tls"],"comments":[{"id":1142,"issue_id":"bd-rth1","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Codex claiming this now. Found a concrete TLS table correctness bug: removing one TID currently breaks linear-probe lookup chains for colliding TIDs, which can corrupt per-thread TLS lookup after teardown. I am implementing tombstone-based delete + collision tests now.","created_at":"2026-02-22T05:19:32Z"},{"id":1143,"issue_id":"bd-rth1","author":"Codex","text":"Completed TLS handoff/teardown hardening and verification.

Implemented:
- Fixed TLS probe-chain deletion correctness in  (tombstone semantics preserve lookup chains).
- Replaced shared fallback TSD with per-thread fallback () to prevent cross-thread leakage for unregistered threads.
- Added ABI integration tests in :
  - 
  - 
  - shared guard + native-threading force helper for deterministic setup.

[31m-    let total_size = GUARD_PAGE_SIZE.checked_add(stack_size).ok_or(crate::errno::ENOMEM)?;
(B[m[32m+    let total_size = GUARD_PAGE_SIZE
(B[m[32m+        .checked_add(stack_size)
(B[m[32m+        .ok_or(crate::errno::ENOMEM)?;
(B[m 
     // Allocate the full region as read+write.
     // SAFETY: anonymous mmap with no fd, valid parameters. ✅
-  ✅
-  ✅
- 
running 36 tests
test pthread::tls::tests::destructor_called_on_teardown ... ok
test pthread::tls::tests::destructor_clears_value_before_calling ... ok
test pthread::tls::tests::destructor_iterates_when_value_reset ... ok
test pthread::tls::tests::destructor_iteration_is_bounded_at_max ... ok
test pthread::tls::tests::no_destructor_means_no_call ... ok
test pthread::tls::tests::destructor_not_called_for_zero_value ... ok

Note: full workspace test sweeps can still hit long-running/flaky hangs in pthread lifecycle/TSD binaries under concurrent workspace load; isolated bead-scope tests above pass consistently.","created_at":"2026-02-22T06:12:14Z"},{"id":1144,"issue_id":"bd-rth1","author":"Codex","text":"Completion summary (clean):

- Fixed TLS probe-chain deletion correctness in `crates/frankenlibc-core/src/pthread/tls.rs` (tombstone semantics preserve collision-chain lookups).
- Replaced shared fallback TSD with per-thread fallback (`thread_local!`) to prevent cross-thread leakage for unregistered threads.
- Added ABI TSD integration coverage in `crates/frankenlibc-abi/tests/pthread_tsd_test.rs`:
  - `tsd_isolated_across_concurrent_threads`
  - `tsd_teardown_keeps_main_thread_clean`
  - serialized native-threading setup helper.

Verification:
- `cargo fmt --check` ✅
- `CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/cargo-target-rth1 cargo check --workspace --all-targets` ✅
- `CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/cargo-target-rth1 cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings` ✅
- `CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/cargo-target-rth1 cargo test -p frankenlibc-core tls::tests -- --nocapture` ✅
- `CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/cargo-target-rth1 cargo test -p frankenlibc-abi --test pthread_tsd_test tsd_isolated_across_concurrent_threads -- --nocapture` ✅
- `CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/cargo-target-rth1 cargo test -p frankenlibc-abi --test pthread_tsd_test tsd_teardown_keeps_main_thread_clean -- --nocapture` ✅

Operational note:
- Full workspace test sweeps can still exhibit intermittent long-running pthread test binaries under heavy concurrent workspace load. Bead-scope TLS tests above pass consistently.

 {"id":"bd-s39o","title":"EPIC: Expand LD_PRELOAD program compatibility beyond 61/61 core suite","description":"FrankenLibC currently passes 61/61 programs in the core LD_PRELOAD test suite PLUS 12+ complex programs (Python, Perl, Node.js, SSH, OpenSSL, Bash, Zsh, Vim, less, nano, curl, make, tar, gzip).

GOAL: Systematically expand compatibility to cover more real-world workloads. The next targets should be programs that exercise features we haven't fully tested:
- Database clients (psql, mysql, sqlite3)
- Build tools (cmake, ninja, gcc, rustc)
- Network tools (wget, dig, nslookup)
- Compression (bzip2, xz, zstd)
- Package managers (apt, dpkg)
- Editors/pagers (emacs, man)

APPROACH: Test each program, capture failure backtraces, fix root causes. Each fix typically benefits many programs.

ALREADY WORKING (verified):
61 core: cat ls echo wc head tail sort grep sed awk date env id uname whoami hostname pwd basename dirname mkdir rmdir touch rm cp mv ln chmod stat file dd tr cut paste fold uniq comm tee xargs yes seq expr test true false printf readlink realpath nproc getconf stty tty logname uptime arch git df du free kill sleep nice
Complex: python3 perl node curl make tar gzip ssh openssl bash zsh vim less nano","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-03-25T03:05:51.740882739Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-25T03:05:51.740882739Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
